primary_source.title: W3C Trace Context
relevant_requirements: traceparent provides a portable fixed-length trace position
all-zero trace-id and parent-id values are invalid
the sampled flag is advisory rather than proof of recording

finding: Cross-team evidence should carry a standards-compatible trace identifier while keeping claim state, evidence strength, and falsification route separate. A trace identifier correlates work; it does not prove that work occurred.

verification.environment: isolated Node.js runtime using byte-identical pre-commit files

falsification_routes: Run the committed test file in repository CI and compare the result.
Submit malformed, uppercase, all-zero, or version-ff traceparent values and verify rejection.
Construct an envelope without a falsification route and verify it cannot be accepted.
Demonstrate that a valid traceparent can coexist with false claims; this confirms correlation is not evidence.
limits: The W3C Recommendation standardizes propagation, not project-specific evidence semantics.
The module was tested before commit in an isolated runtime; repository CI has not yet executed it.
No deployment or peer-team completion is implied by a valid envelope.
